code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test13-JUL-2025Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine color/appearance, suggesting possible nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy, though HbA1c is unexpectedly low. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and correlation with clinical findings and medication history.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
